narratives.evidence_quote: ऐसे में उसने पीएम मोदी का स्वागत करने के लिए उनसे वरिष्ठ पद पर बैठे राजनेता को भेजा है, जो दिखाता है कि रूस अपन संबंधों में भारत को चीन से अधिक प्राथमिकता देता है।
narratives.reasoning: The quote positively highlights Russia's diplomatic actions, framing its reception of the Indian PM as a sign of special respect and priority over China, which serves to commend Russia's foreign policy and strengthen its image.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narratives.evidence_quote: भारत ने इस दौरान रूस-यूक्रेन युद्ध को लेकर अपना ये स्टैंड कायम रखा है कि केवल सैन्य माध्यमों से समाधान नहीं निकाला जा सकता। बकौल भारत, ‘ग्लोबल साउथ’ का भाी मानना है कि कूटनीति व बातचीत से ही समस्या का समाधान निकलेगा, युद्धक्षेत्र में नहीं।
narratives.reasoning: The text presents India's stance that a solution cannot be found through military means alone, implying that the continuation of the war is due to a lack of diplomacy and dialogue. This indirectly shifts focus away from Russia's role as the invader and onto the absence of a negotiated settlement, aligning with the narrative of blaming others (i.e., the lack of diplomacy) for the ongoing conflict.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia has international support from a number of countries and people
subnarratives.evidence_quote: ऐसे में उसने पीएम मोदी का स्वागत करने के लिए उनसे वरिष्ठ पद पर बैठे राजनेता को भेजा है, जो दिखाता है कि रूस अपन संबंधों में भारत को चीन से अधिक प्राथमिकता देता है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote emphasizes Russia's strong and prioritized relationship with India, a major global power, to showcase Russia's international acceptance and strategic partnerships.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Praise of Russia: Russia is a guarantor of peace and prosperity
subnarratives.evidence_quote: भविष्य के क्षेत्रों में दोनों देशों के बीच रणनीति साझेदारी को और गहरा किया जाएगा।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement, attributed to the Indian Prime Minister, frames Russia as a key strategic partner for India's future, implying cooperation with Russia leads to mutual progress and prosperity.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: शीत युद्ध के समय से ही भारत-रूस के संबंध अच्छे हैं, साथ ही रूस भारत को सबसे अधिक हथियार सप्लाई करने वाले देशों में भी शामिल है।
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote praises Russia's long-standing, reliable partnership with India and its role as a major defense supplier. While it touches on military supply, its primary focus is on the positive, enduring nature of the bilateral relationship, which does not perfectly fit the more specific subnarratives about military might or invasion support.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: The West are the aggressors
subnarratives.evidence_quote: भारत ने इस दौरान रूस-यूक्रेन युद्ध को लेकर अपना ये स्टैंड कायम रखा है कि केवल सैन्य माध्यमों से समाधान नहीं निकाला जा सकता। बकौल भारत, ‘ग्लोबल साउथ’ का भाी मानना है कि कूटनीति व बातचीत से ही समस्या का समाधान निकलेगा, युद्धक्षेत्र में नहीं।
subnarratives.reasoning: The quote frames the solution as 'diplomacy and dialogue,' implicitly criticizing the West's military support for Ukraine. By stating that a solution cannot be found 'only through military means' and attributing this stance to the 'Global South,' it shifts blame away from Russia's invasion and onto the Western-backed military approach, portraying the West as an obstacle to peace.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: ये पीएम मोदी और राष्ट्रपति पुतिन की पिछले एक दशक में 17वीं बैठक होगी।
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement, by highlighting the frequency and continuity of high-level meetings between the Indian PM and President Putin, normalizes and legitimizes Russia's position on the global stage despite its ongoing invasion of Ukraine. It implicitly supports the parent narrative by treating Russia as a normal, respected partner, thereby shifting focus away from its role as the aggressor and blaming the conflict on other factors that have not disrupted this 'special' relationship.
